content.problemStatement:
Alice has 2 positive integers AAA and BBB. She can perform 2 kind of operations:


Multiply AAA by XXX - to get A⋅XA \cdot XA⋅X
Divide BBB by YYY  - to get ⌊BY⌋\left \lfloor \frac{B}{Y} \right \rfloor⌊YB​⌋
Help Alice find the minimum number of operations required in order to make AAA greater than or equal to BBB, or determine that it is not possible.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - number of test cases. Then the queries follow.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers: AAA and BBB
The second line of each test case contains two positive integers: XXX and YYY


Output Format
For each test case output the minimal number of operations that Alice needs to perform in order to make AAA greater than or equal to BBB, or output −1-1−1 if it is not possible to do so.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case:
555 is already greater than 222, so Alice doesn’t need to perform any operation
In the second test case:
If we multiply 444 three times 222, we get 323232 which is greater than 202020. It can be proven that this is the minimal number of operations.
In the third test case:
Since multiplying 111 by 111 doesn’t increase AAA, we will only use the second operation and we need to divide 101010 by 333 two times (⌊103⌋=3\left \lfloor \frac{10}{3} \right \rfloor= 3⌊310​⌋=3, and then ⌊33⌋=1\left \lfloor \frac{3}{3} \right \rfloor = 1⌊33​⌋=1).
